headline: Inference and token economics broke out as the field turned to the cost and pricing of running models at scale.
body: Inference led the movers and broke out alongside two new entrants, tokenomics and token usage analysis, as attention shifted to the cost structure of serving models, with TechCrunch framing a possible "Tokenpocalypse" of rising token prices driven by upcoming AI IPOs [0]. Alignment, the control problem, and AI safety all gained as The Economist asked whether AI may soon exceed human control [7]. Economic impact stayed prominent on labor anxiety, including an engineer's account of LLMs eroding software careers [9] and a note on how AI coding ROI differs for bootstrapped founders [6]. Search, SWEbench, and evals weakened over the window.

cited_items.title: Is this the dawn of the Tokenpocalypse?
The ROI of AI coding looks different when you are a bootstrapped founder
Will artificial intelligence soon escape human control? - The Economist
LLMs are eroding my software engineering career and I don't know what to do
